results.question: Which field tells whether the response is the newest version instead of just showing a cache hit?

topItems.title: Aurora Header Notes
topItems.heading: Data Version Field
topItems.sectionPath: Aurora Header Notes > Data Version Field
topItems.text:
[Heading] Data Version Field

Use `data-version` when you need to decide whether the response is the newest snapshot. This field is the right signal for freshness and version comparison.

results.question: Which failure means the caller should fix the request payload before trying again, rather than doing backoff retry?

topItems.title: Aurora Failure Playbook
topItems.heading: Validation Failure
topItems.text:
[Heading] Validation Failure

Return `422` when required fields are missing, types are wrong, or the payload format is invalid. The caller should fix the request content before trying again. This is not a backoff retry case.

citations.title: Aurora Failure Playbook
citations.snippet: [Heading] Validation Failure Return 422 when required fields are missing, types are wrong, or the payload format is invalid. The caller should fix the request content before trying...

results.question: Which failure means the source is not trusted, so the next step is checking credentials or signing instead of changing business fields?

topItems.title: Aurora Failure Playbook
topItems.heading: Signature Failure
topItems.text:
[Heading] Signature Failure

Return `401` when signature verification fails. This means the request source is not trusted, so the next step is to inspect credentials or the signing process instead of changing business fields.

citations.title: Aurora Failure Playbook
citations.snippet: ...n signature verification fails. This means the request source is not trusted, so the next step is to inspect credentials or the signing process instead of changing business fields.

results.question: Which failure usually fits backoff retry because the request can still be valid?

topItems.title: Aurora Failure Playbook
topItems.heading: Downstream Timeout
topItems.text:
[Heading] Downstream Timeout

Return `504` when a dependency does not finish in time. The request itself can still be valid, so this case usually fits backoff retry.
